name: PENDING: Exhaustion Accumulation (2024 10-level, -2 per level on d20 tests)
description:
PENDING MECHANIC: CombatRules exhaustion consumption. 2024 rules: exhaustion has 10 levels. Each level imposes -2 penalty to all d20 tests (attack rolls, saves, checks) and -1 to spell save DC. Level 10 = death.

This scenario applies exhaustion via DM override and then verifies that a subsequent attack roll shows the penalty. The engine currently stores `Character.exhaustion` as a field but does NOT apply the penalty to d20 results.

To make this pass: wire exhaustion into the attack/save/check pipeline: roll.d20 + modifiers - (2 * exhaustionLevel). Also: reduce spell save DC by exhaustionLevel. Also: at exhaustion level 10, set character HP to 0 and mark dead.

actions.comment: === Apply 3 levels of Exhaustion via DM override. ===
PENDING: attack roll should be (d20 - 6) + 7 vs AC 13. Submit d20=10. With exhaustion: 10 - 6 + 7 = 11 → miss. Without exhaustion: 10 + 7 = 17 → hit.
Expect hit=false (with 3 exhaustion: 10 - 6 + 7 = 11 < AC 13). Currently will fail — reports hit=true because exhaustion penalty is not applied.
